title: Strobilanthes zenkeriana
taxonomy: Strobilanthes zenkeriana is a flowering plant species in the Acanthaceae family, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as acanthus or thorny flowers. This family is part of the Lamiales order, a group of flowering plants that also includes mint, basil, and honeysuckle. The Acanthaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, and Strobilanthes zenkeriana is classified within the Acantheae subfamily. Within the Acantheae subfamily, Strobilanthes zenkeriana is a member of the tribe Rupestres, a group of plants that are often found in rocky or mountainous areas. The exact classification of Strobilanthes zenkeriana is not universally agreed upon and may vary depending on the source or authority.

intro: Strobilanthes zenkeriana, commonly known as the African blue sage or blue dragon, is a plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae. Traditionally used in African folk medicine, the plant is valued for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can aid in treating various ailments such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Strobilanthes zenkeriana include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
